title: QA Runtime ODD Monitor FAIL — documents the no-leakage-observed failure mode that ODD_LEAK is designed to catch
_fixture_purpose: Documented FAIL fixture. Demonstrates the failure mode that ODD_LEAK is designed to catch: all declared (sigma, seed) classifier_test_cases produced FP+FN=0, leaving the leakage claim unsupported (no empirical evidence that the continuous baseline misclassifies). This can happen at extremely low σ (e.g., σ=0.005) on a singleton ODD that's far from any boundary in the embedding. The fixture body declares such a case and the fail_ledger documents what ODD_LEAK would have emitted. Mirrors cert [194] csm_fail_wrong_agency.json convention.

exact_membership.method: Deterministic orbit-family-membership monitor on the singleton singularity ODD = {(9,9)}; FP=0/FN=0 holds trivially.

classifier_test_cases.comment: Deliberately low σ — too small to perturb any state out of its orbit-class equivalence class; classifier matches the deterministic monitor exactly.

fail_ledger.message: Deliberately wrong: all declared classifier_test_cases at σ=0.005 produced FP+FN=0, leaving the leakage claim unsupported. If result were not FAIL, the validator would emit: 'ODD_LEAK: no declared (sigma, seed) case produced classifier FP+FN > 0; the leakage claim requires at least one case where the continuous classifier baseline misclassifies'. The cert is unsupported on this declared regime — pick larger σ values or a denser ODD to demonstrate leakage.
